title: Inspect-target controller protection phase
summary: Lane 1 should protect the new route-feel win by extracting support-biased inspect-target selection and its small debug export farther out of game.ts before the next tactile pass grows.

execution_order.goal: Prepare the inspect-target helper extraction handoff.
execution_order.why_now: The route-feel win is real, but the coordinator is still too large to safely absorb another support pass.

execution_order.goal: Extract the support-biased inspect-target seam into a dedicated helper or controller.
execution_order.why_now: Future route-feel work should extend a stable seam, not grow another wrapper cluster inside game.ts.

execution_order.goal: Review the helper extraction for responsibility and safety.
execution_order.why_now: The split only matters if it reduces coordinator risk without changing live behavior.

guardrails: Do not introduce a new support system or planner shell.
Keep the seam small, testable, and route-focused.
Preserve the existing Thaw Window and Held Sand live behavior exactly.

preferred_shape: move support-biased nearest-inspectable selection into field-request-controller or a tiny sibling helper
keep the debug export close to that same seam
leave broader route progress, filing, and overlay behavior where it already lives
avoid: a second wrapper cluster inside game.ts
touching unrelated biome or station logic
